meta.value:
Available tools:

[CRYPTO]
  analyze_cipher(ciphertext: string) — Full analysis of ciphertext: encoding, IoC, auto-decode attempts
  frequency_analysis(text: string) — Letter frequency analysis + Caesar brute force
  detect_cipher_type(text: string) — Heuristic detection of cipher type (base64, hex, morse, RSA, Caesar, Vigenère…)
  run_crypto_solver(ciphertext: string, cipher_type: string (optional, default=auto)) — Automatically solve common ciphers (morse, binary, base*, caesar)
  test_common_keys(ciphertext: string) — Brute-force single-byte XOR and test common weak keys
  rsa_small_exponent(n: string, e: string, c: string) — RSA small exponent and factorisation attacks

[FILESYSTEM]
  read_file(path: string) — Read and display the content of any text file
  list_directory(path: string) — List directory contents recursively (2 levels)
  search_files(pattern: string, directory: string (optional)) — Find files matching a glob pattern
  write_file(path: string, content: string) — Write text content to a file
  run_shell(command: string) — Run a whitelisted shell command (file/strings/xxd/grep/cat/ls…)

[FORENSICS]
  analyze_file_type(file_path: string) — Identify file type via file, magic bytes, binwalk
  extract_metadata_forensics(file_path: string) — Extract all metadata with exiftool
  scan_disk_image(image_path: string) — Analyse disk image: partitions, strings, binwalk
  extract_archives(file_path: string, output_dir: string (optional)) — Extract compressed archives (.zip .tar .gz .7z .rar)
  binwalk_extract(file_path: string) — Deep binwalk extraction with recursive carving
  analyze_pcap(file_path: string) — Full PCAP analysis: protocol summary, endpoints, packets
  pcap_summary(file_path: string) — Quick PCAP statistics via capinfos
  extract_http_streams(file_path: string) — Extract HTTP payloads and objects from PCAP
  extract_dns_queries(file_path: string) — Extract DNS queries from PCAP
  reassemble_files(file_path: string) — Reassemble files from TCP/protocol streams in PCAP

[INTERNET]
  web_search(query: string, max_results: int (optional, default=5)) — Search the web for CTF writeups, techniques and security resources
  fetch_webpage(url: string) — Fetch and return the text content of a webpage by URL
  extract_web_text(url: string) — Fetch URL and extract main article/writeup content
  search_ctf_writeups(challenge_name: string, category: string (optional), keywords: string (optional)) — Targeted search for CTF writeups on ctftime, GitHub, and blogs
  summarize_writeup(url: string) — Fetch a writeup URL and extract the key solution technique

[PWN]
  run_binary(binary_path: string, input_data: string (optional)) — Execute a binary with stdin input
  checksec(binary_path: string) — Check binary security mitigations (NX, PIE, RELRO, canary)
  generate_pwntools_exploit(binary_path: string, vulnerability: string (optional)) — Generate a pwntools exploit template (buffer_overflow|format_string|ret2libc|rop_chain)
  fuzz_binary(binary_path: string, input_dir: string (optional)) — Set up AFL++ fuzzing for a binary
  symbolic_execution(binary_path: string, find_str: string (optional)) — Use angr symbolic execution to find flag-producing inputs
  crash_analysis(binary_path: string, crash_input: string (optional)) — Analyse a crashing input in GDB to determine crash site
  find_rop_gadgets(binary_path: string) — Find ROP gadgets in a binary via ROPgadget
  cyclic_offset(binary_path: string, pattern_length: string (optional, default=200)) — Generate cyclic pattern to find buffer overflow offset

[REVERSE]
  strings(binary_path: string) — Extract printable strings from any file (min length 6)
  disassemble(binary_path: string, function: string (optional, default=main)) — Disassemble a function in a binary using radare2 (default: main)
  analyze_binary(binary_path: string) — Full static analysis: file type, security flags, symbols, imports, strings
  decompile_function(binary_path: string, function: string (optional, default=main)) — Decompile a binary function to pseudo-C via radare2/ghidra
  find_cross_references(binary_path: string, symbol: string) — Find all cross-references to a symbol or address in a binary
  ltrace_run(binary_path: string, args: string (optional)) — Run binary and trace library calls to observe runtime behaviour
  objdump_headers(binary_path: string) — Display ELF section and program headers via objdump

[STEG]
  analyze_image(file_path: string) — Comprehensive image analysis: format, metadata, strings, binwalk
  steg_extract(file_path: string, password: string (optional)) — Extract hidden data using steghide (JPEG/BMP/WAV)
  check_lsb(file_path: string) — Check for LSB steganography using zsteg or PIL
  run_stegsolve(file_path: string, plane: string (optional, bit plane 0-7)) — Bit-plane and colour-channel analysis (StegSolve-style)
  extract_metadata(file_path: string) — Extract all metadata with exiftool (-a -u -g)
  audio_steg(file_path: string) — Analyse audio file for steganography and embedded data
  binwalk_carve(file_path: string) — Extract embedded files from any binary/image using binwalk

[WEB]
  send_http_request(method: string, url: string, headers: json string (optional), body: string (optional), cookies: json string (optional)) — Send any HTTP request (GET/POST/etc) and see response
  crawl_website(url: string, depth: int (optional, default=1)) — Spider a website to discover linked URLs
  discover_endpoints(url: string, wordlist: string (optional)) — Brute-force directories/endpoints with gobuster/dirb
  test_sql_injection(url: string, param: string (optional), method: string (optional)) — Test for SQL injection using sqlmap
  test_xss(url: string, param: string (optional)) — Test reflected XSS with common payloads
  test_command_injection(url: string, param: string (optional), method: string (optional)) — Test for OS command injection with common payloads
  analyze_http_response(response_text: string) — Extract comments, hidden fields, JWT tokens and flags from HTML
